data.name: TUnit0062Description
data.value: The Factory, Services, and HttpCapture properties are not available in constructors or SetupAsync. These properties are initialized in the [Before(HookType.Test)] hook which runs after SetupAsync. Access these properties only in test methods or [Before(HookType.Test)]/[After(HookType.Test)] hooks.

data.name: TUnit0062MessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' cannot be accessed in {1}. It is not initialized until after SetupAsync completes.

data.name: TUnit0063Description
data.value: Do not access Services, Server, or CreateClient on GlobalFactory directly. GlobalFactory is a shared template - use the Factory property instead, which provides an isolated instance for each test.

data.name: TUnit0063MessageFormat
data.value: Do not access '{0}' on GlobalFactory. Use 'Factory.{0}' instead to ensure test isolation.

data.name: TUnit0063Title
data.value: GlobalFactory member access breaks test isolation

data.name: TUnit0064Description
data.value: Classes inheriting directly from Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing.WebApplicationFactory<T> silently lose distributed tracing propagation, per-test logging correlation, and TestContext.Current resolution inside request handlers. Inherit from TUnit.AspNetCore.TestWebApplicationFactory<T> (or wrap an existing factory with TracedWebApplicationFactory<T>) to restore these behaviours.

data.name: TUnit0064MessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' inherits directly from WebApplicationFactory<T>. Inherit from TestWebApplicationFactory<T> to preserve tracing, log correlation, and TestContext.Current.

data.name: TUnit0064Title
data.value: Inherit from TestWebApplicationFactory<T> instead of WebApplicationFactory<T>
